data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 3. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-a7999f14">
    <title>agent: create execute-bead evidence bundles inside attempt worktree</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
execute-bead evidence bundles are still created from the project-root checkout, so live workers leave untracked `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` directories in the main worktree. This is the main-tree noise the coordinator should eliminate: the evidence bundle belongs to the attempt worktree and should land through the same candidate/merge path as the implementation evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1517-1522 calls `createArtifactBundle(projectRoot, wtPath, attemptID)`, passing the main project root as the bundle root even though the attempt worktree path is available.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1632-1648 builds `DirAbs` under `rootDir/.ddx/executions/<attempt>`, so the current call site writes into the main checkout.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:90-96 documents `LandRequest.EvidenceDir` as a project-root relative directory that Land later commits as a trailing evidence commit.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:815-856 has `copyEvidenceDirForLanding(projectRoot, landingWD, relPath)`, which copies evidence from the main project root into a landing worktree when they are different.
- cli/internal/server/workers.go:767-770 calls `VerifyCleanWorktree(projectRoot, res.AttemptID)` as a safety net for leftover evidence, proving the server path still expects project-root evidence dirt.

PROPOSED FIX
- Create the per-attempt bundle under `wtPath/.ddx/executions/<attempt>` for normal execute-bead attempts.
- Ensure prompt, manifest, result, usage, checks, and any review metadata are part of the worker/candidate evidence that the coordinator can inspect and commit.
- Change landing to consume evidence from the candidate/landing worktree for normal attempts instead of copying from the main checkout.
- Preserve evidence for no-land/no-change/no-evidence outcomes intentionally, but do it through a controlled publish/copy path that leaves the main worktree clean after the attempt finalizes.
- Keep durable `.ddx/executions` evidence tracked when the attempt lands; eliminate untracked live-worker evidence noise in the main checkout.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not delete historical `.ddx/executions` records.
- Do not change candidate review, repair, or routing policy.
- Do not remove the existing evidence format during the `.ddx/runs` migration window.

PARENT + DEPS
Parent: ddx-31e07fdc. Depends on ddx-7cb3153d because project-root candidate refs provide the durable reference point for worktree-owned evidence during review, repair, and recovery.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_BundleCreatedInAttemptWorktree proves `prepareArtifacts` creates `.ddx/executions/<attempt>` under the isolated attempt worktree, not under the main project root.
2. TestExecuteBeadWork_MainTreeHasNoUntrackedExecutionEvidence runs a successful fake attempt and asserts `git status --short` in the main checkout has no untracked `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` entry while the attempt is in normal finalization.
3. TestLand_EvidenceCommitUsesWorktreeBundleWithoutProjectCopy proves Land commits the worktree-owned evidence bundle and does not need `copyEvidenceDirForLanding` from the project root on the normal path.
4. TestExecuteBead_NoLandPreservesEvidenceWithoutMainNoise proves no-change/no-evidence/no-land outcomes preserve the bundle through an explicit publish path and leave the main worktree clean afterward.
5. Existing evidence bundle readers still load prompt, manifest, result, usage, and checks for both historical project-root bundles and new worktree-origin bundles.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... -run "TestExecuteBeadArtifacts_BundleCreatedInAttemptWorktree|TestExecuteBeadWork_MainTreeHasNoUntrackedExecutionEvidence|TestLand_EvidenceCommitUsesWorktreeBundleWithoutProjectCopy|TestExecuteBead_NoLandPreservesEvidenceWithoutMainNoise" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/server/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:evidence, area:git, kind:bug, reliability, prevention, spec:FEAT-010, candidate-cycle</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-31e07fdc" base-rev="56d528de2a807f3dc7c3c4a6de61c2f75f15cc0b" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260508T043844-9139bbb4"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All 4 AC tests pass. The implementation is complete:

- **`createArtifactBundle`** now places `DirAbs` inside `wtPath` (not `rootDir`), eliminating untracked `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` noise in the main checkout during execution
- **`commitEvidenceBundleInWorktree`** commits the bundle as a trailing commit in the attempt worktree on the success path, so `Land()` finds it without needing `copyEvidenceDirForLanding`
- **`publishEvidenceBundleToProjectRoot`** (deferred, runs before `WorktreeRemove`) handles no-land paths by copying from wtPath→projectRoot
- **`landEvidence`** detects already-committed evidence via `evidenceDirHasTrackedFiles`, skipping the re-commit
- **`synthesizeCommitExcludePathspecs`** now excludes prompt.md, manifest.json, result.json, usage.json so they don't get picked up as code changes
- **Worktree-lost recovery** checks `os.Stat(wtPath)` *before* `writeArtifactJSON` (which would recreate the directory via `os.MkdirAll`), then writes recovery stubs to projectRoot directly
